_license: Public SIPRI Top-100 arms-industry dataset (Stockholm International Peace Research Institute). Free to use with attribution. Cite https://www.sipri.org/databases/armsindustry.

entries.summary: SIPRI Top-100 rank #1: ~$60.8B arms revenue (2023), ~88% of total company revenue. World's largest arms producer.
SIPRI Top-100 rank #2: ~$40.6B arms revenue (2023), ~59% of total revenue. Includes Raytheon, Pratt & Whitney, Collins Aerospace.
SIPRI Top-100 rank #3: ~$35.2B arms revenue (2023), ~89% of total company revenue. B-21 Raider stealth bomber prime.
SIPRI Top-100 rank #4: ~$30.5B arms revenue (2023), ~39% of total revenue (rest = commercial aviation). Defense segment includes F-15EX, KC-46, Apache.
SIPRI Top-100 rank #5: ~$33.7B arms revenue (2023), ~78% of total revenue. Submarines, combat vehicles, IT services for DoD.
SIPRI Top-100 rank #6: ~$29.6B arms revenue (2023), ~97% of total revenue. Largest EU defence contractor.
SIPRI Top-100 rank #11: ~$17.6B arms revenue (2023), ~84% of total revenue. Defense electronics, comms, ISR.
SIPRI Top-100 rank #8: ~$14.6B arms revenue (2023), ~83% of total revenue. Italy's primary defence contractor.
SIPRI Top-100 rank #12: ~$12.0B arms revenue (2023), ~16% of total revenue (rest = commercial aviation).
SIPRI Top-100 rank #13: ~$5.6B arms revenue (2023), ~15% of total revenue. Defense segment is aerospace + warfighter solutions.
SIPRI Top-100 rank #14: ~$11.9B arms revenue (2023), ~55% of total revenue. France's primary defence-electronics group.
SIPRI Top-100 rank #15: ~$9.8B arms revenue (2023), ~66% of total revenue. Major DoD IT services contractor.
SIPRI Top-100 rank #17: ~$10.1B arms revenue (2023), ~95% of total revenue. US Navy's primary shipbuilder.
SIPRI Top-100 rank #20: ~$6.2B arms revenue (2023), ~67% of total revenue. Major DoD / IC consulting firm.
SIPRI Top-100 rank #23: ~$5.9B arms revenue (2023), ~79% of total revenue. DoD IT and systems-engineering services.
SIPRI Top-100 rank #19: ~$6.0B arms revenue (2023), ~32% of total revenue. Military aero engines, naval reactors.
SIPRI Top-100 rank #21: ~$4.7B arms revenue (2023), ~35% of total revenue. Bell helicopters, military aircraft.
SIPRI Top-100 rank #22: ~$4.7B arms revenue (2023), ~13% of total revenue. Japan's primary defence contractor.
SIPRI Top-100 rank #24: ~$5.8B arms revenue (2023), ~95% of total revenue. Israel's largest non-state arms exporter.
SIPRI Top-100 rank #25: ~$2.6B arms revenue (2023), ~51% of total revenue. Norwegian defence + maritime.
SIPRI Top-100 rank #26: ~$4.6B arms revenue (2023), ~88% of total revenue. Sweden's primary defence contractor — Gripen fighter, GlobalEye.
SIPRI Top-100 rank #27: ~$2.6B arms revenue (2023), ~96% of total revenue. US arm of Italian Leonardo.
SIPRI Top-100 rank #31: ~$3.9B arms revenue (2023), ~59% of total revenue. DoD / IC IT services.
SIPRI Top-100 rank #30: ~$3.2B arms revenue (2023), ~43% of total revenue. Major DoD logistics + engineering services.
SIPRI Top-100 rank #33: ~$4.6B arms revenue (2023), ~18% of total revenue. Military propulsion, equipment.
SIPRI Top-100 rank #47: ~$2.7B arms revenue (2023), ~55% of total revenue. UK naval engineering + defence support.
SIPRI Top-100 rank #58 (Textron subsidiary): military rotorcraft (V-280 Valor, AH-1Z, UH-1Y).
SIPRI Top-100 rank #7: ~$22.1B arms revenue (2023). China's largest aircraft + defence-aero conglomerate.
SIPRI Top-100 rank #9: ~$22.1B arms revenue (2023). China's largest land-systems + ammunition producer.

entries.brand: China Aerospace Science and Industry Corporation (CASIC)
entries.summary: SIPRI Top-100 rank #10: ~$20.0B arms revenue (2023). Chinese missile + space systems.
